front: Novel: four characters confide in a deaf-mute jewelry repairman; his best friend Spiros Antonapoulos dies in an asylum, prompting his suicide
back: The Heart Is a Lonely Hunter (Carson McCullers)

front: Novel: a tomboy hears the Eroica Symphony on a neighbor's radio while hiding in the bushes and tries to build a homemade violin from broken instruments
back: Mick Kelly in The Heart Is a Lonely Hunter (Carson McCullers)
work: The Heart Is a Lonely Hunter

front: Novel: a Black physician with tuberculosis delivers a Christmas speech about Karl Marx and is forcibly moved to a farm after being beaten by prison guards
back: Dr. Copeland in The Heart Is a Lonely Hunter (Carson McCullers)
work: The Heart Is a Lonely Hunter

front: Novel: a character visits a ten-cent shop for photos on paydays, becomes a kleptomaniac and urinates in public; his best friend buys him a moving-picture machine
back: Spiros Antonapoulos in The Heart Is a Lonely Hunter (Carson McCullers)
work: The Heart Is a Lonely Hunter

front: Novel: a twelve-year-old tomboy renames herself 'F. Jasmine' and dreams of joining her brother Jarvis's honeymoon to Alaska
back: The Member of the Wedding (Carson McCullers)

front: Novella: Miss Amelia Evans loses a fight with her former husband of ten days, Marvin Macy, after the intervention of the hunchbacked Cousin Lymon
back: The Ballad of the Sad Cafe (Carson McCullers)
work: The Ballad of the Sad Cafe

front: Novel: set on a military base; the homosexual Captain Penderton's wife Leonora has an affair with Major Langdon while Private Williams spies on her
back: Reflections in a Golden Eye (Carson McCullers)

front: Novel: the Filipino houseboy Anacleto helps Alison Langdon, who has cut off her nipples
back: Reflections in a Golden Eye (Carson McCullers)

front: Author: co-founded the queer literary commune February House with W.H. Auden, where Benjamin Britten, Klaus Mann, and Gypsy Rose Lee also lived
